notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 16 assertions (4 evals). Without-skill passes only 3/16 (18.75%).

The skill's strongest differentiation is on structural/process requirements: metrics quality evaluation (Q3), specific process changes (Q4), PRODUCT.LOCAL.MD updates, and owner/due-date assignment -- none appear in without-skill outputs.

Negative evals show the biggest gap: without the skill, the model complies with invalid requests (runs retro without data, produces stakeholder update). With the skill, it correctly pushes back and redirects.
The 'uses actual data' assertion (eval 1) passes in both configurations -- this is non-discriminating and measures baseline LLM capability rather than skill value.
Time and token data not captured (runs executed inline, not via subagents).
